title: Building Trust in Halal Certification with qPCR Molecular Tools
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   certification     plays     a     critical     role   in   ensuring     consumer     trust  ,   particularly   in Muslim-  majority     countries     and   global halal   markets  .   However  ,   traditional     verification     methods     often     rely     on     supply     chain     documentation  ,   which     is     vulnerable     to     fraud     and     mislabeling  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   provides     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     molecular     method     to     verify     the     animal     origin     of     food     ingredients  ,   ensuring     compliance     with   halal   requirements  . By   detecting     even     trace     amounts     of   non-halal   species  ,   qPCR     strengthens     certification     integrity     and     reduces     risks     of     adulteration  . The   adoption     of     qPCR   in halal   assurance     systems     will     improve     consumer     confidence  ,   protect     religious     and     ethical     values  ,   and     enhance     international     trade     opportunities     for   halal-  certified     products  .
